name: FormaCollection CRUD API

references.text: Item creation protocol: IFormaItem with static createForParent(parentId: UUID64, cfg): T - each item type handles its own creation logic
FormaCollection constructor: constructor(parentId: UUID64) - immutable, items unaware of parent, cascade delete handled at parent level
ID generation: addItem calls createRelation(parentId) to generate item's UUID64 ID
API: items(): T[] returns shallow copy of internal array; size: number getter for collection size
Item lifecycle: IFormaItem protocol handles initialization and validation via createForParent(parentId, cfg)
Storage: Internal array of T in insertion order, items accessed by ID via find/filter
Design Decision: FormaCollection encapsulates array, sole owner pattern - items created via addItem factory, no external mutations
Error handling: deleteItem(id) returns undefined if ID not found; patchItem(id, cfg) throws if ID not found
CRUD API: addItem(cfg): T, deleteItem(id): T|undefined, getItem(id): T|undefined, patchItem(id, cfg): T|undefined, moveItem(id, toIndex): T|undefined, items(): T[], size: number
CRUD API update: Rename updateItem → patchItem to clarify partial update semantics (cfg applies only specified fields, ID immutable)
moveItem signature: moveItem(id: UUID64, {before?: UUID64, after?: UUID64}): T - before: null=insert, after: null=append, neither=append. Throws if item ID or anchor ID not found
Refactoring: rename forma-array.ts to forma-collection.ts, update all imports and test files

summary: Design and implement generic CRUD methods (addItem/deleteItem/getItem/moveItem/updateItem) for FormaCollection to standardize managed, ordered collection management across Forma classes
